---
title-block-banner: images/banner.png
title: "Scam Compound Explorer"
subtitle: "Remote Sensing Detection of Suspected Scam Compounds in Southeast Asia"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    toc-location: left
    toc-expand: true
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full
    grid:
      sidebar-width: 250px
      body-width: 900px
      margin-width: 100px
    css: scam.css
---

## Project Summary

This project develops a Google Earth Engine (GEE)–based application for researchers and human rights monitors to systematically identify and explore spatial patterns of suspected scam compounds in Southeast Asia, specifically within Cambodia. This model, referencing data from Amnesty International(2025) and ASPI, extracts remote sensing indicators of involuntary detention infrastructure to identify high-risk areas. Using 53 confirmed locations as a reference sample, the project employs a similarity-based analysis method to detect 45 suspicious locations with similar physical characteristics. This dual approach allows for a rigorous comparison between verified sites of human rights abuse and uninvestigated candidate locations. The interactive web platform enhances spatial communication and supports collaborative documentation, and help governments and law enforcement agencies combat criminal gangs to some extent, and better protect national border security.

---

### Problem Statement

Scam compounds are difficult to systematically detect due to:

- Limited verified ground-truth data
- Rapid spatial development in border regions
- Overlap with legitimate urban and commercial environments
- Inconsistent reporting across NGO and governmental sources

This project supports researchers and policy-oriented users by providing a reproducible,
satellite-based screening tool grounded in documented reference cases, rather than
making definitive claims of confirmed presence.

### End User

Our platform is designed for human rights monitoring agencies, investigative journalists,
and policy-oriented researchers working to combat transnational crime in Southeast Asia.
Limited public information and complex on-the-ground situations have long hampered the
effectiveness of international interventions. Our interactive maps significantly enhance
data accessibility and applicability, fostering collaboration among global stakeholders
and providing robust spatial evidence to support international initiatives against forced
labour, human trafficking, and torture.

---

### Data

<!-- TODO: 数据组补充完整数据说明 -->

| Category | Dataset | Description | Source |
|----------|---------|-------------|--------|
| Satellite Imagery | Sentinel-2 SR Harmonised | Multispectral imagery for NDBI, NDVI and visual basemap (2021–2024) | [GEE Catalog](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/COPERNICUS_S2_SR_HARMONIZED) |
| Night-time Lights | VIIRS DNB Monthly | Night-time radiance for activity growth detection (dNTL 2021–2024) | [GEE Catalog](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/NOAA_VIIRS_DNB_MONTHLY_V1_VCMSLCFG) |
| Boundaries | USDOS LSIB Simple 2017 | Country boundary geometries for AOI definition | [GEE Catalog](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/USDOS_LSIB_SIMPLE_2017) |
| Boundaries | FAO GAUL 2015 | Province-level administrative boundaries | [GEE Catalog](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/FAO_GAUL_2015_level1) |
| Reference Sites | Confirmed Scam Locations | 53 confirmed sites from Amnesty International and ASPI | [Amnesty International](https://www.amnesty.org) |
| Reference Sites | Suspected and Control Points | 45 suspected sites and 22 control points for validation | [ASPI](https://www.aspi.org.au) |

---

### Methodology

<!-- TODO: 分析组补充方法说明 -->

The analysis pipeline operates in two stages:

**Stage 1 — Satellite Embedding Similarity**

A Sentinel-2 median composite (2024, 20 m resolution) is used to compute per-pixel
spectral similarity against the 53 confirmed reference sites. Areas exceeding the p97
similarity threshold are retained as Stage 1 candidates, covering approximately 3.7%
of the Cambodia–Vietnam AOI while capturing all suspected sites and non-reference
confirmed sites within 500 m.

$$\text{Similarity} = \frac{\sum_{i} w_i \cdot \cos(\mathbf{e}_i, \mathbf{r}_i)}{\sum_{i} w_i}$$

**Stage 2 — Indicator-Based Refinement**

Candidate zones are scored against a metrics stack:

| Indicator | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| dNDBI 2021–2024 | Built-up area growth: $\text{NDBI} = \frac{\text{SWIR} - \text{NIR}}{\text{SWIR} + \text{NIR}}$ |
| dNTL 2021–2024 | Night-time light radiance growth |
| dNDVI 2021–2024 | Vegetation loss |
| dist_to_confirmed_m | Proximity to known confirmed sites |
| dist_to_border_m | Proximity to national borders |

---

### Interface

<!-- TODO: 截图完成后替换占位说明和图片路径 -->

The application provides a split-panel layout with an interactive map on the right
and an analysis dashboard on the left. Key interface components include:

:::: {.columns layout-align="center"}
::: {.column width="46%"}
![Main Map View](images/screenshot_map.png)

*Candidate density heatmap over the Cambodia–Vietnam AOI with confirmed and
suspected site markers.*
:::
::: {.column width="2%"}
:::
::: {.column width="46%"}
![AOI Dashboard](images/screenshot_dashboard.png)

*AOI Dashboard showing candidate tier distribution.*
:::
::::

---

## The Application

<!-- TODO: 替换为发布后的App链接 -->

::: {.column-page}
<iframe src='https://code.earthengine.google.com/YOUR_CODE_LINK_HERE'
        width='100%' height='700px'></iframe>
:::

---

## How it Works

The application is built entirely in Google Earth Engine and operates through a
two-stage screening pipeline. Below are key code examples illustrating the core logic.

**Stage 1: Computing NDBI Change**

```js
// Compute NDBI for 2021 and 2024 Sentinel-2 composites
var s2_2021 = ee.ImageCollection('COPERNICUS/S2_SR_HARMONIZED')
  .filterBounds(aoi)
  .filterDate('2021-01-01', '2021-12-31')
  .filter(ee.Filter.lt('CLOUDY_PIXEL_PERCENTAGE', 30))
  .select(['B11', 'B8'])
  .median();

var ndbi2021 = s2_2021.normalizedDifference(['B11', 'B8']).rename('NDBI');
var ndbi2024 = s2_2024.normalizedDifference(['B11', 'B8']).rename('NDBI');

// dNDBI: positive values indicate built-up growth
var dNDBI = ndbi2024.subtract(ndbi2021).rename('dNDBI');
```

**Stage 2: Candidate Tier Classification**

```js
// Candidates are scored and classified into tiers
// High: both development_flag and activity_flag triggered
// Medium: one flag triggered  
// Low: neither flag, retained as lower-confidence candidates
var highCandidates   = candidates.filter(ee.Filter.eq('priority_tier', 'high'));
var mediumCandidates = candidates.filter(ee.Filter.eq('priority_tier', 'medium'));
var lowCandidates    = candidates.filter(ee.Filter.eq('priority_tier', 'low'));
```

**Candidate Density Visualisation**

```js
// Paint candidate polygons at 1 km scale and apply smoothing kernel
var painted = ee.Image().byte().paint(candidates, 1)
  .setDefaultProjection('EPSG:3857', null, 1000);

var kernel = ee.Kernel.square({radius: 12000, units: 'meters', normalize: false});

var densityImage = painted
  .reduceNeighborhood({reducer: ee.Reducer.sum(), kernel: kernel, optimization: 'boxcar'})
  .resample('bilinear')
  .clip(aoi)
  .selfMask();
```

The Stage 2 pipeline reduces 24,464 Stage 1 candidates to a shortlist of 15
high-priority zones by applying distance and night-time light thresholds:

| Tier | Count | Criteria |
|------|-------|----------|
| High | 8,425 | Both development and activity flags triggered |
| Medium | 13,059 | One flag triggered |
| Low | 2,980 | Neither flag |
| **Operational shortlist** | **15** | **Strictest distance and NTL conditions** |

![Stage 2 Refinement Pipeline](images/methodology_diagram.png)

---

## References

<!-- TODO: 全组补充完整参考文献 -->

- Amnesty International (2023). *Trapped: The Human Suffering behind the Scam Centres*.
  London: Amnesty International.

- Australian Strategic Policy Institute (2023). *Mapping the Scam Compound Industry
  in Southeast Asia*. Canberra: ASPI.

- Gorelick, N., Hancher, M., Dixon, M., Ilyushchenko, S., Thau, D. and Moore, R.
  (2017). Google Earth Engine: Planetary-scale geospatial analysis for everyone.
  *Remote Sensing of Environment*, 202, pp.18–27.
  https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rse.2017.06.031

- Amnesty International (2025). ‘I was someone else’s property’: Slavery, human trafficking and torture in Cambodia’s scamming compounds. [online] Available at: https://www.amnesty.org/en/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/ASA2394472025ENGLISH.pdf [Accessed 25 Apr. 2026].

  
<table style="background-color:#60a5fa; width:100vw; margin-left:calc(-50vw + 50%); margin-top:80px; padding:0 40px; box-sizing:border-box; border:none;">
  <tr>
    <td style="color:#eff6ff; font-size:12px; text-align:left; border:none; padding:15px 0;">CASA0025 Group Project</td>
    <td style="color:#eff6ff; font-size:12px; text-align:right; border:none; padding:15px 0;">For all precomputation scripts and main app code, please visit: <a href="https://github.com/你们的仓库链接" style="color:#eff6ff;">GitHub Repository</a></td>
  </tr>
</table>
